list.name: Changes
list.comment:
● Yes, there are a few behavioral changes that could break code relying on the old (incorrect) behavior:
1. frappe.user is now an object, not a string • Before: frappe.user == "Administrator" worked. • Now: frappe.user.name == "Administrator" is required, matching real Frappe. • Any custom shim code or scripts treating frappe.user as a string will need updating.
2. Error response body shape changed for ORM/validation/Python errors • Before: REST endpoints returned {"error": "..."} (or {"message": "..."} for reportview) with various HTTP statuses. • Now: Python/validation/permission/auth errors return Frappe-shaped JSON: {"exc": "...", "exc_type": "...", "_server_messages": "[]"} with HTTP 200, matching what Frappe JS expects. • Any non-Frappe client manually parsing the old error/message field for these endpoints will need to handle the new shape. • RuntimeError::NotFound in get_doc/update_doc still returns HTTP 404, but the body is now Frappe-shaped rather than {"error": ...}.
3. Filter operators now actually work • Before: {"field": ["in", ["a","b"]]} was treated as equality against the array (effectively a no-match). • Now: it generates a real IN clause. This fixes wrong/empty results, but any client that accidentally relied on the no-op behavior will see different data.
The core API contracts (endpoint URLs, request payloads, successful response shapes) are unchanged. These are correctness fixes that align Kiff with real Frappe semantics, but they are breaking if you had code depending on the previous incorrect implementations.

task.summary: Default task

Remove IntelliJ IDEA project configuration files
- Deleted redundant `.idea` directory files including data sources, configurations, and other metadata. - Removed project-specific settings to clean up the repository.

Sync apps.json with Cargo.toml manifests
- Align `Cargo.toml` dependencies and workspace members with `apps.json` configuration. - Replace `sebrus_logger` with `strongroom` in runtime and workspace configurations. - Update build script to automate syncing between `apps.json` and Cargo manifests.

Add email connectivity module and extend permission merging logic
- Introduced a new `crates/email` module for handling SMTP/IMAP connectivity, configuration, and testing. Includes `test_email_settings`, `send_email`, and `fetch_inbox` methods for email operations. - Extended the PermissionEngine to merge `docperm`, `__kiff_docperm`, and `custom_docperm` permissions with precedence, ensuring runtime permissions respect user configuration and seeded defaults. - Created tables for `docperm` and `custom_docperm` to align with extended merging behavior. - Updated `.idea` project files to reflect new module additions.

Add email connectivity module and extend permission merging logic
- Introduced a new `crates/email` module for handling SMTP/IMAP connectivity, configuration, and testing. Includes `test_email_settings`, `send_email`, and `fetch_inbox` methods for email operations. - Extended the PermissionEngine to merge `docperm`, `__kiff_docperm`, and `custom_docperm` permissions with precedence, ensuring runtime permissions respect user configuration and seeded defaults. - Created tables for `docperm` and `custom_docperm` to align with extended merging behavior. - Updated `.idea` project files to reflect new module additions.

Add `audit_ready` app for server token management
Introduced the `audit_ready` app for managing infrastructure server tokens, including UI, HTTP handlers, and backend processes. Updated `sync_rust_apps.py` to support case-insensitive app directory resolution and adjusted project files accordingly.

Add initial Playwright E2E test setup for AuditReady Compliance
Created a `package.json` file to define the Playwright end-to-end testing framework with essential scripts and dependencies. This prepares the foundation for running and managing E2E tests for the AuditReady Compliance runtime.

Add initial Playwright E2E test setup for AuditReady Compliance
Created a `package.json` file to define the Playwright end-to-end testing framework with essential scripts and dependencies. This prepares the foundation for running and managing E2E tests for the AuditReady Compliance runtime.

Add initial Playwright E2E test setup for AuditReady Compliance
Created a `package.json` file to define the Playwright end-to-end testing framework with essential scripts and dependencies. This prepares the foundation for running and managing E2E tests for the AuditReady Compliance runtime.

Add Playwright E2E tests for Infrastructure Server and Kubernetes Cluster creation
Introduced end-to-end tests to validate the creation and persistence of Infrastructure Servers and Kubernetes Clusters via the UI, ensuring backend data integrity and expected UI behavior.

Delete outdated server token codebase
Removed legacy code related to server token handling, including APIs, handlers, UI components, and infrastructure logic. This paves the way for a more streamlined and updated token management approach going forward.
fixed bugs
fixed bugs
Enhance OAuth handling with improved error logging and input validation

Handle missing `redirect_uri` for custom OAuth providers
Inject fallback logic to dynamically construct `redirect_uri` when it is not included in the provider configuration, ensuring compatibility with custom/injected OAuth providers.

Improve OAuth `redirect_uri` handling for custom providers
Added fallback logic to build an absolute `redirect_uri` when missing or relative, ensuring compatibility with custom/injected providers and satisfying requirements like Microsoft's absolute URI enforcement.

Refactor OAuth provider resolution and key mapping
Simplified and optimized the logic for resolving OAuth provider types, Social Login Keys, and configuration keys. Improved error handling, logging, and provider compatibility checks. Removed redundant fallback code for provider configuration.

Improve logging and error handling for initialization paths
Enhanced initialization logic for the crash-durable log engine in Rust and added comprehensive fallback mechanisms for building redirect_uri in Python. Improved debug logging in both cases to aid troubleshooting and clarify error contexts.

Refactor OAuth `redirect_uri` logic for better request context handling
Simplified the construction of `redirect_uri` by prioritizing request headers and context over fallback mechanisms. Ensured compatibility with Microsoft Entra ID by enforcing absolute URI requirements. Streamlined error handling for edge cases.
Enhance OAuth `redirect_uri` handling with improved fallback logic and detailed debug logging
Enhance OAuth `redirect_uri` handling with improved fallback logic and detailed debug logging
Enhance OAuth `redirect_uri` handling with improved fallback logic and detailed debug logging

Refactor OAuth provider mapping and enhance Microsoft tenant configuration
Streamlined the logic for resolving OAuth provider keys and Social Login Keys. Added support to dynamically configure tenant-specific Microsoft OAuth endpoints using tenant IDs from environment or config. Improved compatibility by rewriting `/common/` endpoints to tenant-specific URLs.

Refactor OAuth provider mapping and enhance Microsoft tenant configuration
Streamlined the logic for resolving OAuth provider keys and Social Login Keys. Added support to dynamically configure tenant-specific Microsoft OAuth endpoints using tenant IDs from environment or config. Improved compatibility by rewriting `/common/` endpoints to tenant-specific URLs.

Add enhanced logging and tenant-specific handling for OAuth providers
Improved debug and warning logs for OAuth provider flows. Added logic to rewrite `/common/` Microsoft OAuth endpoints to tenant-specific URLs. Enhanced flow configuration to ensure compatibility and accuracy of authorization and token exchange mechanisms.
Add detailed debug logging for OAuth provider resolution and Social Login Key matching steps
Add detailed debug logging for OAuth provider resolution and Social Login Key matching steps
Add `restrict_to_domain` field to Doctype and enhance workspace child-table handling

Add support for page fixtures and extend infrastructure UI
Introduced handling for page fixtures in `sync_all()` to enable better alignment with permission checks and native pages. Updated infrastructure workspace and frontend screens to include new shortcuts and links, such as "Server Token Generator" and "SEBRUS::OPS" pages. Enhanced database handling for pages with additional fields like `restrict_to_domain`.

Update log engine data files and index metadata
Refreshed log entries in `wal.ndjson`, updated `.managed.json` with new index files, and modified `meta.json` to include new segments.

Add workspace redirects and enhance fleet UI filtering
Introduced redirects for custom workspace pages in Rust runtime to align with new URL structure. Enhanced fleet UI with status-based filtering and visibility counts, improving user experience in Ops Portal. Updated log engine data files and index metadata to reflect recent changes in logs and segments.

Implement batch processing of logs and optimize vulnerability scans.
Add batch ingestion to reduce write overhead for high-frequency logs. Optimize vulnerability scans by batching database writes, skipping unchanged servers, and enhancing OSV concurrency limits. Also introduce telemetry and heartbeat caching for improved performance.

Implement batch processing of logs and optimize vulnerability scans.
Add batch ingestion to reduce write overhead for high-frequency logs. Optimize vulnerability scans by batching database writes, skipping unchanged servers, and enhancing OSV concurrency limits. Also introduce telemetry and heartbeat caching for improved performance.

Add in-memory caches for telemetry and job handling.
Introduced RAM-based caches for patch jobs, software inventories, and log staging to reduce SQLite contention and enhance system responsiveness. Updated APIs and the log engine to leverage these caches for high-frequency tasks, ensuring consistent, real-time performance while persisting data asynchronously.

Refactor and optimize batch processing in core components
Revised batch processing logic across multiple modules to reduce write contention and improve performance. Introduced chunked operations, environment-variable-driven configurations, and logic to cap resource usage dynamically, ensuring better scalability under heavy loads.

Refactor code for improved readability and formatting.
Enhanced code readability by reformatting multi-line constructs such as match arms, function calls, and array definitions. Applied consistent indentation and alignment to enhance maintainability and prepare for future contributions.

Optimize database pool management and enhance vulnerability data handling.
Added self-healing mechanisms for wedged SQLite pools to prevent data corruption and improve reliability. Introduced shared fetch caches and payload capping for vulnerability alerts in the frontend to enhance performance and reduce server load.

Remove auto-generated server token logic
Eliminated legacy code for auto-creating tokens during server or client insertion, reinforcing explicit token generation via the API for better security and consistency. Updated related tests and SQL filtering, and added new token-related unit tests for validation.

Add tunnel agent reconnection logic to Ops Portal
Introduced an automatic reconnection mechanism that waits for the tunnel agent to come online before establishing a WebSocket connection. Enhanced user feedback with updated status indicators and streamlined session cleanup to handle reconnection scenarios effectively.

Add diagnostics for agent connection attempts in Ops Portal
Extended server response to include details on the most recent agent connection attempt when no agent is connected. Added support for tracking connection attempt timestamps, success status, and error messages in `BrokerState`. Updated the frontend to display this information for better debugging and user feedback.

"Implement WebSocket keepalive ping for idle timeout prevention
Added a periodic keepalive ping mechanism to maintain active WebSocket connections by preventing idle timeouts. Adjusted the connection interval and idle task management to ensure dead connections are reaped while live connections remain responsive. Updated related documentation to explain the new behavior."

Add per-server activity cache and node-level network/process views
Introduced an in-memory cache for per-server process and network activity to enable real-time data retrieval. Enhanced the frontend with live network monitoring and process inspection for individual nodes, including efficient renders for large data sets and dynamic view updates.

● Yes, there are a few behavioral changes that could break code relying on the old (incorrect) behavior:
1. frappe.user is now an object, not a string • Before: frappe.user == "Administrator" worked. • Now: frappe.user.name == "Administrator" is required, matching real Frappe. • Any custom shim code or scripts treating frappe.user as a string will need updating.
2. Error response body shape changed for ORM/validation/Python errors • Before: REST endpoints returned {"error": "..."} (or {"message": "..."} for reportview) with various HTTP statuses. • Now: Python/validation/permission/auth errors return Frappe-shaped JSON: {"exc": "...", "exc_type": "...", "_server_messages": "[]"} with HTTP 200, matching what Frappe JS expects. • Any non-Frappe client manually parsing the old error/message field for these endpoints will need to handle the new shape. • RuntimeError::NotFound in get_doc/update_doc still returns HTTP 404, but the body is now Frappe-shaped rather than {"error": ...}.
3. Filter operators now actually work • Before: {"field": ["in", ["a","b"]]} was treated as equality against the array (effectively a no-match). • Now: it generates a real IN clause. This fixes wrong/empty results, but any client that accidentally relied on the no-op behavior will see different data.
The core API contracts (endpoint URLs, request payloads, successful response shapes) are unchanged. These are correctness fixes that align Kiff with real Frappe semantics, but they are breaking if you had code depending on the previous incorrect implementations.

MESSAGE.value:
Refactor OAuth `redirect_uri` logic for better request context handling
Simplified the construction of `redirect_uri` by prioritizing request headers and context over fallback mechanisms. Ensured compatibility with Microsoft Entra ID by enforcing absolute URI requirements. Streamlined error handling for edge cases.
Enhance OAuth `redirect_uri` handling with improved fallback logic and detailed debug logging

Refactor OAuth provider mapping and enhance Microsoft tenant configuration
Streamlined the logic for resolving OAuth provider keys and Social Login Keys. Added support to dynamically configure tenant-specific Microsoft OAuth endpoints using tenant IDs from environment or config. Improved compatibility by rewriting `/common/` endpoints to tenant-specific URLs.

Add enhanced logging and tenant-specific handling for OAuth providers
Improved debug and warning logs for OAuth provider flows. Added logic to rewrite `/common/` Microsoft OAuth endpoints to tenant-specific URLs. Enhanced flow configuration to ensure compatibility and accuracy of authorization and token exchange mechanisms.
Add detailed debug logging for OAuth provider resolution and Social Login Key matching steps
Add `restrict_to_domain` field to Doctype and enhance workspace child-table handling

Add support for page fixtures and extend infrastructure UI
Introduced handling for page fixtures in `sync_all()` to enable better alignment with permission checks and native pages. Updated infrastructure workspace and frontend screens to include new shortcuts and links, such as "Server Token Generator" and "SEBRUS::OPS" pages. Enhanced database handling for pages with additional fields like `restrict_to_domain`.

Update log engine data files and index metadata
Refreshed log entries in `wal.ndjson`, updated `.managed.json` with new index files, and modified `meta.json` to include new segments.

Add workspace redirects and enhance fleet UI filtering
Introduced redirects for custom workspace pages in Rust runtime to align with new URL structure. Enhanced fleet UI with status-based filtering and visibility counts, improving user experience in Ops Portal. Updated log engine data files and index metadata to reflect recent changes in logs and segments.

Implement batch processing of logs and optimize vulnerability scans.
Add batch ingestion to reduce write overhead for high-frequency logs. Optimize vulnerability scans by batching database writes, skipping unchanged servers, and enhancing OSV concurrency limits. Also introduce telemetry and heartbeat caching for improved performance.

Add real-time RAM caching for log ingestion and fleet status.
Introduced an in-memory staging buffer for log records to enhance ingestion performance by reducing disk writes. Extended fleet status and patch job handling to leverage RAM caches for real-time updates, minimizing SQLite queries and improving responsiveness across both APIs and the UI.

Add in-memory caches for telemetry and job handling.
Introduced RAM-based caches for patch jobs, software inventories, and log staging to reduce SQLite contention and enhance system responsiveness. Updated APIs and the log engine to leverage these caches for high-frequency tasks, ensuring consistent, real-time performance while persisting data asynchronously.

Add dynamic configuration and improve batching efficiency
Introduce environment variable-based overrides for various parameters, such as log engine heap size, max SQLite connections, batch sizes, and intervals, to enhance configurability. Refactor existing bulk operations and background tasks to process in chunks, reducing contention and improving performance. Additionally, adjust defaults for more resource-efficient operation on constrained systems.

Refactor and optimize batch processing in core components
Revised batch processing logic across multiple modules to reduce write contention and improve performance. Introduced chunked operations, environment-variable-driven configurations, and logic to cap resource usage dynamically, ensuring better scalability under heavy loads.

Refactor code for improved readability and formatting.
Enhanced code readability by reformatting multi-line constructs such as match arms, function calls, and array definitions. Applied consistent indentation and alignment to enhance maintainability and prepare for future contributions.

Optimize database pool management and enhance vulnerability data handling.
Added self-healing mechanisms for wedged SQLite pools to prevent data corruption and improve reliability. Introduced shared fetch caches and payload capping for vulnerability alerts in the frontend to enhance performance and reduce server load.

Remove auto-generated server token logic
Eliminated legacy code for auto-creating tokens during server or client insertion, reinforcing explicit token generation via the API for better security and consistency. Updated related tests and SQL filtering, and added new token-related unit tests for validation.

Add tunnel agent wait loop for remote shell connections
Introduced automatic retries for connecting to disconnected tunnel agents. Updated shell status display to differentiate between "CONNECTING" and "WAITING FOR AGENT" states. Refactored tunnel connection logic for improved maintainability.

Add tunnel agent reconnection logic to Ops Portal
Introduced an automatic reconnection mechanism that waits for the tunnel agent to come online before establishing a WebSocket connection. Enhanced user feedback with updated status indicators and streamlined session cleanup to handle reconnection scenarios effectively.

Add diagnostic tracking for agent connection attempts
Introduced `AgentAttempt` to track and surface diagnostic details about agent connection attempts, aiding server admins in debugging connection issues. Updated Ops Portal to display the last attempt's status and message. Enhanced broker logic to record both successful and failed attempts, with relevant details shared in API responses.

Add diagnostics for agent connection attempts in Ops Portal
Extended server response to include details on the most recent agent connection attempt when no agent is connected. Added support for tracking connection attempt timestamps, success status, and error messages in `BrokerState`. Updated the frontend to display this information for better debugging and user feedback.

"Implement WebSocket keepalive ping for idle timeout prevention
Added a periodic keepalive ping mechanism to maintain active WebSocket connections by preventing idle timeouts. Adjusted the connection interval and idle task management to ensure dead connections are reaped while live connections remain responsive. Updated related documentation to explain the new behavior."

Add per-server activity cache and node-level network/process views
Introduced an in-memory cache for per-server process and network activity to enable real-time data retrieval. Enhanced the frontend with live network monitoring and process inspection for individual nodes, including efficient renders for large data sets and dynamic view updates.

Add toggle for node processes visibility in ops portal
Introduced a toggle button to show/hide the node processes block in the node panel, enhancing session-level user preferences. Updated related CSS for a smaller button size and adjusted the HTML structure to accommodate visibility states. Runtime log file was extended, and a minor "opstamp" value change was made in the meta.json file.

● Yes, there are a few behavioral changes that could break code relying on the old (incorrect) behavior:
1. frappe.user is now an object, not a string • Before: frappe.user == "Administrator" worked. • Now: frappe.user.name == "Administrator" is required, matching real Frappe. • Any custom shim code or scripts treating frappe.user as a string will need updating.
2. Error response body shape changed for ORM/validation/Python errors • Before: REST endpoints returned {"error": "..."} (or {"message": "..."} for reportview) with various HTTP statuses. • Now: Python/validation/permission/auth errors return Frappe-shaped JSON: {"exc": "...", "exc_type": "...", "_server_messages": "[]"} with HTTP 200, matching what Frappe JS expects. • Any non-Frappe client manually parsing the old error/message field for these endpoints will need to handle the new shape. • RuntimeError::NotFound in get_doc/update_doc still returns HTTP 404, but the body is now Frappe-shaped rather than {"error": ...}.
3. Filter operators now actually work • Before: {"field": ["in", ["a","b"]]} was treated as equality against the array (effectively a no-match). • Now: it generates a real IN clause. This fixes wrong/empty results, but any client that accidentally relied on the no-op behavior will see different data.
The core API contracts (endpoint URLs, request payloads, successful response shapes) are unchanged. These are correctness fixes that align Kiff with real Frappe semantics, but they are breaking if you had code depending on the previous incorrect implementations.

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value:
● Yes, there are a few behavioral changes that could break code relying on the old (incorrect) behavior:
1. frappe.user is now an object, not a string • Before: frappe.user == "Administrator" worked. • Now: frappe.user.name == "Administrator" is required, matching real Frappe. • Any custom shim code or scripts treating frappe.user as a string will need updating.
2. Error response body shape changed for ORM/validation/Python errors • Before: REST endpoints returned {"error": "..."} (or {"message": "..."} for reportview) with various HTTP statuses. • Now: Python/validation/permission/auth errors return Frappe-shaped JSON: {"exc": "...", "exc_type": "...", "_server_messages": "[]"} with HTTP 200, matching what Frappe JS expects. • Any non-Frappe client manually parsing the old error/message field for these endpoints will need to handle the new shape. • RuntimeError::NotFound in get_doc/update_doc still returns HTTP 404, but the body is now Frappe-shaped rather than {"error": ...}.
3. Filter operators now actually work • Before: {"field": ["in", ["a","b"]]} was treated as equality against the array (effectively a no-match). • Now: it generates a real IN clause. This fixes wrong/empty results, but any client that accidentally relied on the no-op behavior will see different data.
The core API contracts (endpoint URLs, request payloads, successful response shapes) are unchanged. These are correctness fixes that align Kiff with real Frappe semantics, but they are breaking if you had code depending on the previous incorrect implementations.
